title: Codex review verdict
description: The machine-readable verdict Codex emits as its final message for each review round (via --output-schema, written by -o/--output-last-message). It is the ONLY output channel: the human-readable review lives in review_markdown (there is no separate prose-on-stdout channel — stdout under --output-schema/--json is JSON, not prose). The allOf enforces the load-bearing invariants: APPROVE iff blocking_issues empty; REVISE has >=1; REJECT must carry justification. The orchestrator treats any inconsistent/invalid verdict as an error to re-prompt once then escalate (never CONVERGED).

decision.description: APPROVE = converged (blocking_issues empty). REVISE = >=1 blocking issue, iterate. REJECT = fundamental flaw iteration won't fix, escalate to human.

confidence.description: REQUIRED (it is load-bearing): an APPROVE with confidence below cfg.min_confidence[stage] downgrades to awaiting_human instead of auto-advancing (DESIGN §7). Set the threshold to 0 to disable that gate while still recording confidence.

review_markdown.description: The full human-readable review, Markdown. The orchestrator renders reviews/<stage>-<round>-review.md from this field. This is the prose channel — do NOT expect prose on stdout.

summary.description: One-paragraph plain-language verdict (a short digest of review_markdown).

blocking_issues.description: Issues that MUST be fixed before approval. Empty iff decision is APPROVE (enforced by allOf).

id.description: Short id, e.g. B1, B2. NOTE: ids are emitted fresh by an independent reviewer each round and are NOT stable across rounds; the orchestrator must not key oscillation/regression logic on them (DESIGN §10).

location.description: REQUIRED for blockers: where in the artifact (section / file:line). Used with title to derive the orchestrator's stable content key for oscillation detection.

suggested_fix.description: A PROPOSAL, not a command. The author addresses the underlying concern and may deviate (noting why) if the literal fix would be unsafe or out of scope.

non_blocking_suggestions.description: Nice-to-haves ('nits') that do not block approval and never add a round.

reject_reason.description: Why the artifact should be redone rather than patched. Required for REJECT unless the fundamental flaw is itemized in blocking_issues (enforced by allOf).

addressed_previous.description: IDs of prior-round blocking issues the reviewer judges resolved this round. Advisory only: validated by the orchestrator against the actual prior verdict's ids before being trusted.
